title: More Nonsense Limerick 72
content:
There was an old person of Blythe,
    Who cut up his meat with a scythe;
    When they said, "Well! I never!" 
    He cried, "Scythes for ever!"
    That lively old person of Blythe.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edward Lear, titled "More Nonsense Limerick 72", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
